en: And the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying to them,
zh: 耶和華對摩西和亞倫說：

en: "Speak to the people of Israel, saying, These are the living things that you may eat among all the animals that are on the earth.
zh: “你們要告訴以色列人：地上所有的走獸中，你們可以吃的動物，就是這些：

en: Whatever parts the hoof and is cloven-footed and chews the cud, among the animals, you may eat.
zh: 分蹄有趾而且反芻的走獸，你們都可以吃。

en: Nevertheless, among those that chew the cud or part the hoof, you shall not eat these: The camel, because it chews the cud but does not part the hoof, is unclean to you.
zh: 但你們不可吃下列反芻，或分蹄的走獸動物：駱駝，因為牠反芻卻不分蹄，你們應以為不潔淨。

en: And the rock badger, because it chews the cud but does not part the hoof, is unclean to you.
zh: 石貛 ，因為牠反芻卻不分蹄，你們應以為不潔淨。

en: And the hare, because it chews the cud but does not part the hoof, is unclean to you.
zh: 兔子，因為牠反芻卻不分蹄，你們應以為不潔淨。

en: And the pig, because it parts the hoof and is cloven-footed but does not chew the cud, is unclean to you.
zh: 豬，因為牠分蹄有趾卻不反芻，你們應以為不潔淨。

en: You shall not eat any of their flesh, and you shall not touch their carcasses; they are unclean to you.
zh: 這些走獸的肉你們不可吃，牠們的屍體你們不可摸，你們應視為不潔淨。

en: "These you may eat, of all that are in the waters. Everything in the waters that has fins and scales, whether in the seas or in the rivers, you may eat.
zh: “水中所有的活物你們可以吃的，就是這些：凡是在水中，有翅有鱗的，不論是在海裡或是河裡的，你們都可以吃。

en: But anything in the seas or the rivers that has not fins and scales, of the swarming creatures in the waters and of the living creatures that are in the waters, is detestable to you.
zh: 在水中游動或生存在水中的活物，無論是在海裡或是河裡，若是沒有翅和鱗的，你們都要當作可憎之物。

en: You shall regard them as detestable; you shall not eat any of their flesh, and you shall detest their carcasses.
zh: 牠們是你們憎惡之物；牠們的肉，你們不可吃；牠們的屍體，你們要憎惡。

en: Everything in the waters that has not fins and scales is detestable to you.
zh: 所有在水裡沒有翅和鱗的活物，你們都要當作可憎之物。

en: "And these you shall detest among the birds; they shall not be eaten; they are detestable: the eagle, the bearded vulture, the black vulture,
zh: “在鳥類中，你們要憎惡，也不可吃這些雀鳥：兀鷹 、鵰、鷲、

en: the kite, the falcon of any kind,
zh: 鳶、獵鷹等隼類，

en: every raven of any kind,
zh: 所有烏鴉類，

en: the ostrich, the nighthawk, the sea gull, the hawk of any kind,
zh: 鴕鳥、貓頭鷹、海鷗、蒼鷺等鷹類。

en: the little owl, the cormorant, the short-eared owl,
zh: 鴟鶚、鸕鷥、大鴟梟、

en: the barn owl, the tawny owl, the carrion vulture,
zh: 白鷺、塘鵝、鴇、

en: the stork, the heron of any kind, the hoopoe, and the bat.
zh: 鸛、紅鶴等鷺鷥類，戴勝和蝙蝠。

en: "All winged insects that go on all fours are detestable to you.
zh: “凡有翅膀、四足爬行的生物，你們都要當作可憎之物。

en: Yet among the winged insects that go on all fours you may eat those that have jointed legs above their feet, with which to hop on the ground.
zh: 但在所有有翅膀、四足爬行的生物中，如果有足有腿，能在地上蹦跳的，你們都可以吃。

en: Of them you may eat: the locust of any kind, the bald locust of any kind, the cricket of any kind, and the grasshopper of any kind.
zh: 在昆蟲中，你們可以吃這些：蝗蟲類、螞蚱類、蟋蟀類和蚱蜢類。

en: But all other winged insects that have four feet are detestable to you.
zh: 但是其他所有有翅膀四足的生物，你們都要當作可憎之物。

en: "And by these you shall become unclean. Whoever touches their carcass shall be unclean until the evening,
zh: “以下事物，會使你們成為不潔：觸摸動物屍體的，就不潔淨到晚上。

en: and whoever carries any part of their carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
zh: 拾起牠們的屍體的，就要洗淨自己的衣服，並且不潔淨到晚上。

en: Every animal that parts the hoof but is not cloven-footed or does not chew the cud is unclean to you. Everyone who touches them shall be unclean.
zh: 所有分蹄卻無趾的、不反芻的走獸，你們應以為不潔淨，觸摸牠們的，就不潔淨。

en: And all that walk on their paws, among the animals that go on all fours, are unclean to you. Whoever touches their carcass shall be unclean until the evening,
zh: 所有用四足行走的動物中，用腳掌行走的，你們應以為不潔淨，觸摸牠們屍體的，都不潔淨到晚上。

en: and he who carries their carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening; they are unclean to you.
zh: 拾起牠們屍體的，就要洗淨自己的衣服，並且不潔淨到晚上。以上這些你們應以為不潔淨。

en: "And these are unclean to you among the swarming things that swarm on the ground: the mole rat, the mouse, the great lizard of any kind,
zh: “在地上爬行的生物中，你們應以為不潔淨的是這些：鼬鼠、鼫鼠、蜥蜴這一類，

en: the gecko, the monitor lizard, the lizard, the sand lizard, and the chameleon.
zh: 以及壁虎、龍子、守宮、蛇醫、變色龍。

en: These are unclean to you among all that swarm. Whoever touches them when they are dead shall be unclean until the evening.
zh: 在所有爬行的生物中，你們應以這些為不潔淨；牠們死了以後，觸摸牠們的，都不潔淨到晚上。

en: And anything on which any of them falls when they are dead shall be unclean, whether it is an article of wood or a garment or a skin or a sack, any article that is used for any purpose. It must be put into water, and it shall be unclean until the evening; then it shall be clean.
zh: 牠們當中死了的，掉在甚麼東西上，無論是木器或是衣服，皮子或是布袋等任何器具，都成為不潔淨，要放在水中。但仍不潔淨到晚上，以後就真潔淨了。

en: And if any of them falls into any earthenware vessel, all that is in it shall be unclean, and you shall break it.
zh: 牠們當中死了的，掉在甚麼瓦器裡，其中不論是甚麼，都成為不潔淨，你們要把瓦器打碎。

en: Any food in it that could be eaten, on which water comes, shall be unclean. And all drink that could be drunk from every such vessel shall be unclean.
zh: 瓦器的水若是滴在任何食物上，食物就成了不潔淨，若是滴在裝了飲料的器皿裡，飲料也成為不潔淨。

en: And everything on which any part of their carcass falls shall be unclean. Whether oven or stove, it shall be broken in pieces. They are unclean and shall remain unclean for you.
zh: 牠們屍體的任何部分掉在甚麼東西上，那東西就不潔淨，無論是爐或是鍋，都成為不潔淨，應該打碎；你們應以這些為不潔淨。

en: Nevertheless, a spring or a cistern holding water shall be clean, but whoever touches a carcass in them shall be unclean.
zh: 但是水源和儲水池仍算是潔淨的。只有觸摸水中屍體的，才是不潔淨。

en: And if any part of their carcass falls upon any seed grain that is to be sown, it is clean,
zh: 牠們屍體的一部分掉在要播種的種子上，種子還是潔淨的；

en: but if water is put on the seed and any part of their carcass falls on it, it is unclean to you.
zh: 但是，如果種子已經澆了水，牠們屍體的一部分才掉在種子上面，你們應以這種子為不潔淨。

en: "And if any animal which you may eat dies, whoever touches its carcass shall be unclean until the evening,
zh: “一隻你們可以吃的走獸死了，誰摸了牠的屍體，就不潔淨到晚上；

en: and whoever eats of its carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening. And whoever carries the carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
zh: 誰吃了牠的屍體，就要洗淨自己的衣服，並且不潔淨到晚上；誰拾起這屍體，也要洗淨自己的衣服，並且不潔淨到晚上。

en: "Every swarming thing that swarms on the ground is detestable; it shall not be eaten.
zh: “所有在地上爬行的動物，都是可憎之物，都不可吃。

en: Whatever goes on its belly, and whatever goes on all fours, or whatever has many feet, any swarming thing that swarms on the ground, you shall not eat, for they are detestable.
zh: 凡是用肚子，或用四足或是多足在地上爬行的動物，你們都不可吃，因為牠們是可憎之物。

en: You shall not make yourselves detestable with any swarming thing that swarms, and you shall not defile yourselves with them, and become unclean through them.
zh: 你們不可因任何爬行的動物使自己成為可憎的，也不可因牠們玷污自己，以致不潔淨。

en: For I am the LORD your God. Consecrate yourselves therefore, and be holy, for I am holy. You shall not defile yourselves with any swarming thing that crawls on the ground.
zh: 因為我是耶和華你們的 神，所以你們要使自己成為聖潔。你們要分別為聖，因為我是聖潔的。你們不可因在地上爬行的任何動物玷污自己，

en: For I am the LORD who brought you up out of the land of Egypt to be your God. You shall therefore be holy, for I am holy."
zh: 因為我是耶和華，曾把你們從埃及地領上來，為要作你們的 神；你們要分別為聖，因為我是聖潔的。”

en: This is the law about beast and bird and every living creature that moves through the waters and every creature that swarms on the ground,
zh: 以上就是有關走獸、飛禽、所有在水中游行的動物，和所有在地上爬行的動物的律例，

en: to make a distinction between the unclean and the clean and between the living creature that may be eaten and the living creature that may not be eaten.
zh: 為要把不潔淨的和潔淨的，可吃的生物和不可吃的生物，分別出來。
